message:
perf(ci): Add benchmark build caching to avoid rebuilding frozen_functions.c

Problem:
- CI rebuilds all benchmarks from scratch on every run
- frozen_functions.c (7.8MB) takes very long to compile
- Each matrix job rebuilds the same benchmarks

Solution:
Cache zig-out/bin/bench/ and zig-out/cache/bench/ directories
- Includes .wasm, .aot, frozen_functions.c, and bundle_compiled.c
- Cache key based on bench/*.js, polyfills, and build.zig
- Invalidates when source changes

Results:
- First run: Full build (slow)
- Subsequent runs: Cached builds (fast)
- Still tests build pipeline when source changes

files.patch:
@@ -43,6 +43,16 @@ jobs:
           install-hyperfine: 'true'
           install-porffor: 'true'
 
+      - name: Cache benchmark builds
+        uses: actions/cache@v4
+        with:
+          path: |
+            zig-out/bin/bench/
+            zig-out/cache/bench/
+          key: benchmark-builds-${{ hashFiles('bench/**/*.js', 'src/polyfills/**/*.js', 'build.zig') }}
+          restore-keys: |
+            benchmark-builds-
+
       - name: Run Benchmark - ${{ matrix.benchmark.title }}
         run: |
           # Script writes to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY automatically
